AGENT-AUTH.
Specification Implementations Examples Registry GitHub

Implementations

SDKs & Libraries

Reference implementations and community libraries for the Agent Auth Protocol. The protocol is simple enough to implement from scratch — Ed25519 keypairs, device authorization flow, signed JWTs, and three database tables.

Reference

Better Auth Plugin

TypeScript / Server / Reference

Full reference implementation as a Better Auth plugin. Drop-in agent identity, registration, device flow, capability management, and session verification.

Agent registrationDevice authorization flowCapability managementSession verificationLifecycle management

Agent Auth SDK

TypeScript / Client / Reference

Client SDK for agent runtimes. Manages keypairs, signs requests, handles registration and capability escalation.

Ed25519 key managementJWT signingRequest binding (DPoP)Auto-registrationScope escalation
Community

Your implementation here

The protocol is open. Implement it in any language and submit a PR to be listed.
Build your own

Implementing from scratch

The protocol is intentionally simple to implement. On the server side, you need three database tables (hosts, agents, agent capabilities), Ed25519 signature verification, and a device authorization flow. On the client side, you need key generation, JWT signing, and HTTP request binding.

What you need
Server: 3 tables + JWT verification + device flow
Client: Ed25519 keygen + JWT signing + request binding
Protocol: DPoP-style (RFC 9449) + Device Auth (RFC 8628)
Read the full specification →

Built on Better Auth
